subject: anatomy-1
title: [FIXTURE] Mock Exam
description: Fixture mock exam — every question carries a pastPaperRef as the schema requires for mock tests.

questions.stem: A fall on an outstretched hand fractures the surgical neck of the humerus. Which structure is most at risk in this fracture?

explanation.text: The axillary nerve and posterior circumflex humeral vessels wrap the surgical neck of the humerus, so a fracture there classically injures the axillary nerve.

questions.stem: The radial nerve passes through the cubital fossa alongside the brachial artery.

explanation.text: False. The cubital fossa contains (lateral to medial) the biceps tendon, brachial artery, and median nerve. The radial nerve lies lateral to the fossa, between brachialis and brachioradialis.

questions.stem: A patient reports numbness over the medial one-and-a-half fingers and weak finger abduction. Which nerve is affected?

explanation.text: The ulnar nerve supplies sensation to the medial 1.5 fingers and motor to most intrinsic hand muscles, including the interossei that abduct/adduct the fingers.
